inputs.reasoning: Named capture group with word character class stresses the named capture parsing and \w class expansion in the compiler.
Nested repetitions trigger special handling in the compiler for potentially ambiguous/exponential structures.
Unicode named property class exercises the Unicode group lookup and compilation paths.
Negated character class with hex escape ranges covering non-ASCII stresses the character class complement logic.
Large counted repetition exercises the repeat-expansion code path in the compiler.
Non-capturing group with alternation and open-ended repetition stresses alternation and repetition compilation.
Mixed inline flag toggling (case-insensitive on/off within subexpressions) exercises flag-scoping in the parser.
Word boundary assertions combined with \w class test the boundary and character-class compilation paths.
Deeply nested capturing groups stress the group numbering and subexpression tracking in the parser/compiler.
Character class mixing multiple Unicode properties (Letter, Number) with literal chars exercises Unicode class union logic.
